values.name: None
values.raw: 0- No appointment should ever have this status.

values.raw: 1- Shows as a regularly scheduled appointment.

values.raw: 5- Shows with a big X on it.

values.raw: 6- Planned appointment.  Only shows in Chart module. User not allowed to change this status, and it does not display as one of the options.

values.raw: 7- Patient "post-it" note on the schedule. Shows light yellow. Shows on day scheduled just like appt, as well as in prog notes, etc.

values.raw: 8- Patient "post-it" note completed

values.name: Normal
values.raw: 0 - Default priority

values.name: ASAP
values.raw: 1 - Used to identify items for the ASAP list

values.name: None
values.raw: No ProcCodes from CodeStrRequired are needed to schedule appointments of this AppointmentType.

values.name: AtLeastOne
values.raw: At least one ProcCode from CodeStrRequired is needed to schedule appointments of this AppointmentType.

values.name: All
values.raw: All ProcCodes from CodeStrRequired are needed to schedule appointments of this AppointmentType.

values.name: Undefined
values.raw: -1 - Used to define an Undefined ApptReminderType.

values.name: Reminder
values.raw: 0 - Used to define the rules for when reminders should be sent out.

values.name: ConfirmationFutureDay
values.raw: 1 - Defines rules for when confirmations should be sent out.

values.name: ReminderFutureDay
values.raw: 2 - DEPRECATED. As of 17.4, all reminders have a status of Reminder.

values.name: PatientPortalInvite
values.raw: 3 - Send emails to patients with their credentials to the Patient Portal.

values.name: ScheduleThankYou
values.raw: 4 - Defines rules for when Schedule Verify ("Thank You"s) should be sent out.

values.name: Arrival
values.raw: 5 - Defines rules for when Arrival instructions should be sent out.

values.name: Birthday
values.raw: 6 - Birthday. Defines rule for sending out automated birthday emails.

values.name: GeneralMessage
values.raw:
7 - General Message. Defines rules for sending out automated messages after an appointment is set complete.
            TSPrior will always be negative (occurs in future) for GeneralMessage. Allowing GM before appointment is prohibited since it would simply be duplicating eReminder behavior.

values.name: WebSchedRecall
values.raw: 8 - WebSchedRecall. (Note, not yet used in db, but could be if we ever want clinic specific templates) Defines rules for sending out automated messages for Recalls.

values.name: NewPatientThankYou
values.raw: 9 - NewPatientThankYou, Thank you for New Patient which is able to send with a new patient web form URL.

values.name: PayPortalMsgToPay
values.raw: 10 - PaymentPortal Msg-To-Pay, used to send msg-to-pay messages to patients. Currently not an AutoComm feature but doing this now so functionality can be easier added in the future.

values.name: EClipboardWeb
values.raw: 11 - EClipboard Web, used to send eClipboard web URLs to existing patients. No automation yet. Sent by right clicking on appt.

values.name: Regular
values.raw: 0 - This is a regular email that may contain our special wiki markup. Not converted to html.

values.name: Html

values.name: RawHtml
values.raw: 2 - More advanced html that does not include the master template. User must provide everything.

values.name: EveryAppointment
values.raw: 1 - Send a patient portal invite every appointment.

values.name: NoVisitInTimespan
values.raw: 2 - Send a patient portal invite if the patient hasn't visited patient portal within TimeSpanMultipleInvites pref.

2 - EConnector has sent this to HQ and will remain in this status until it is either terminated or receives a response from the 
            patient.

values.name: Viewed
values.raw: 3 - The patient viewed the portal and took no action.

values.name: ViewedNotAvailable
values.raw: 4 - The patient viewed the portal but the slot was no longer available.

values.name: AcceptedAndMoved
values.raw: 5 - The patient accepted the appointment and the appointment was successfully moved.

values.name: AcceptedAndNotAvailable

values.name: Declined
values.raw: 7 - The patient declined any open slots.

values.name: ChoseDifferentSlot
values.raw: 8 - The patient declined this slot but chose a different time slot.

values.name: Expired
values.raw: 9 - Patient took no action by the time DateTimeExpired passed and the message was terminated.

values.name: Failed
values.raw: 10 - HQ or EConnector was unable to send the message so it was terminated prematurely.

values.name: DeclinedStopComm
values.raw: 11 - The patient declined and requested that we do not continue contacting them for this appointment.

values.name: PtNoteCompleted
values.raw: 8- Patient "post-it" note completed

values.name: Archived
values.raw: 3 - This status is also used for a merged patient that you're not keeping.

values.name: Prospective
values.raw: 6- Not an actual patient yet.

values.name: NeedsSheet
values.raw: 0- Condition based on if a patient doesn't have a sheet filled.  CompareString stores sheetdef.Description.  Comparison is typically Equals, but Contains can also be used.

values.name: Problem
values.raw: 1- Condition based on if a patient has an active disease.  CompareString stores the diseasedef.DiseaseName.  Contains or Equals is used for Comparison.

values.name: Medication
values.raw: 2- Condition based on if a patient is actively taking a medication.  CompareString stores the medication.MedName.  Contains or Equals is used for Comparison.

values.name: Allergy
values.raw: 3- Condition based on if a patient has an active allergy.  CompareString stores the allergydef.Description.  Contains or Equals is used for Comparison.

values.name: Age
values.raw: 4- Condition based on patient.Age.  Age to compare is stored in CompareString.  Contains, Equals, GreaterThan, and LessThan can be used for Comparison.  This is the only CompareField that can use GreaterThan or LessThan for Comparison.

values.name: Gender
values.raw: 5- Condition based on patient.Gender.  CompareString can store the string values "M" or "F" (not case sensitive).  Comparison can be Contains or Equals, though they both perform the same comparison since the logic only uses the first letter of the patient's gender.  PatientGender.Unknown and PatientGender.Other are not currently supported.

values.name: Labresult
values.raw: 6- Condition based on if a labresult exists for a patient.  CompareString stores labresult.TestName.  Comparison can be Contains or Equals.

values.name: InsuranceNotEffective
values.raw: 7- Condition based on if a patient's lowest ordinal insurance plan is not effective.  CompareString not used.  Comparison=None.

values.raw: 3: The only place that this status is used is to make adjustments to benefits from the coverage window.  It is never attached to a claim.

values.name: Supplemental
values.raw: 4:This differs from Received only slightly.  It's for additional payments on procedures already received.  Most fields are blank.

values.name: CapClaim
values.raw: 5: CapClaim is used when you want to send a claim to a capitation insurance company.  These are similar to Supplemental in that there will always be a duplicate claimproc for a procedure. The first claimproc tracks the copay and writeoff, has a status of CapComplete, and is never attached to a claim. The second claimproc has status of CapClaim.

values.name: Estimate
values.raw: 6: Estimates have replaced the fields that were in the procedure table.  Once a procedure is complete, the claimprocstatus will still be Estimate.  An Estimate can be attached to a claim and status gets changed to NotReceived.

values.name: CapComplete
values.raw: 7: For capitation procedures that are complete.  This replaces the old procedurelog.CapCoPay field. This stores the copay and writeoff amounts.  The copay is only there for reference, while it is the writeoff that actually affects the balance. Never attached to a claim. If procedure is TP, then status will be CapEstimate.  Only set to CapComplete if procedure is Complete.

values.name: CapEstimate
values.raw: 8: For capitation procedures that are still estimates rather than complete.  When procedure is completed, this can be changed to CapComplete, but never to anything else.

values.name: CognitiveResult
values.raw: 1 - This clinical statement contains details of an evaluation or assessment of a patient’s cognitive status. The evaluation may include assessment of a patient's mood, memory, and ability to make decisions. The statement will include, if present, supporting caregivers, non-medical devices, and the time period for which the evaluation and assessment were performed.

values.name: CognitiveProblem
values.raw: 2 - A cognitive status problem observation is a clinical statement that describes a patient's cognitive condition, findings or symptoms. Examples of cognitive problem observations are inability to recall, amnesia, dementia, and aggressive behavior. A cognitive problem observation is a finding or medical condition. This is different from a cognitive result observation, which is a response to a question that provides insight to the patient's cognitive status. It reflects findings that provide information about a medical condition, while a result observation reflects responses to questions in a cognitive test or those that provide information about a person's judgement, comprehension ability, and response speed.

values.name: FunctionalResult
values.raw: 3 - This clinical statement represents details of an evaluation or assessment of a patient’s functional status. The evaluation may include assessment of a patient's  language, vision, hearing, activities of daily living, behavior, general function, mobility and self-care status. The statement will include, if present, supporting caregivers, non-medical devices, and the time period for which the evaluation and assessment were performed.

values.name: FunctionalProblem
values.raw: 4 - A functional status problem observation is a clinical statement that represents a patient’s functional perfomance and ability.

values.name: MedicationList
values.raw: 7-A Medication List is a complex field. It consists of a list of medications with an optional second column for strength and frequency. Each medication has a Delete button to its right. There is also an Add button and a None checkbox at the bottom. The None checkbox only shows when the list is empty and allows satisfying a 'required' flag. There is no way to indicate 'no changes', but the office is free to add a separate No Changes checkbox below this list which doesn't actually do anything but which can serve as a visual indicator.

values.name: PatientList
values.raw: 8-This is a complex field that includes a popup window. On the form, it shows as a read-only textbox with a button beside it that says 'Pick'. The popup window has a list of patients that are pulled from the live office db in real time. In that window, there's also an Add button which actually adds a new patient to the office database. For now, there's no mechanism to edit existing patients in that list. There's also a second popup window to enter the name and BD of a new patient. The only use so far is to pick insurance plan subscriber, but more uses are planned such as guarantor, guardian, adding children to family, etc.

values.name: InsPick
values.raw: 9-Shows as 3 radio buttons: None, New, and Existing. Selecting Existing makes a popup window show to allow selecting an existing insurance in the family.

values.raw: 4 WebMail received from patient portal.  Shows in OD Inbox and in pt Chart module.  Also shows in PP as a sent and unread WebMail msg.

values.raw: 5 WebMail received from patient portal that has been marked read.  Shows in the OD Inbox and in pt Chart module.  Also shows in PP as a sent and read WebMail.

values.raw: 6 Webmail sent from provider to patient.  Shows in Chart module and also shows in PP as a received and unread WebMail msg.

values.raw: 7 Webmail sent from provider to patient and read by patient.  Shows in Chart module and also shows in PP as a received and read WebMail msg.

values.name: SentDirect
values.raw: 8 Sent and encrypted using Direct. Required for counting messages in EHR modules g.1 and g.2, Automated Measure Calculation.

values.name: ReceivedEncrypted
values.raw: 9 Received email matches application/pkcs7-mime mime type, but could not be decrypted.  Shows in Inbox.  The user can decrypt from FormEmailMessageEdit.  If the user has the correct private key, then the status will change to Read.

values.name: ReceivedDirect
values.raw: 10 Received email matches application/pkcs7-mime mime type and has been decrypted.  Shows in Inbox.  Once it's attached to a patient it will also show in Chart module.  When viewing inside of FormEmailMessageEdit, the XML body of the message shows as xhtml instead of raw.  Still need to work on supporting collapsing and expanding, as required for meaningful use in 2014.

values.name: ReadDirect
values.raw: 11 For received direct messages.  Has been read.  Shows in Inbox.  Once it's attached to a patient it will also show in Chart module.  When viewing inside of FormEmailMessageEdit, the XML body of the message shows as xhtml.

values.name: AckDirectProcessed
values.raw: 12 Message Delivery Notification (MDN) processed.  Always outgoing.  Indicates to sender that a Direct message was received and decrypted, but not necessarily displayed for the user.  Does not show in patient Chart.  Attached to the same patient as the incoming email which caused the MDN to be sent.

values.name: AckDirectNotSent
values.raw:
13 Message Delivery Notification (MDN) created and saved to db, but not sent yet.  Does not show in patient Chart.  Attached to the same patient as the incoming email which caused the MDN to be created.
            This status is used to try resending MDNs if they fail to send.  The MDN is saved to the db so the unset MDNs can be found easily, and also because MDNs are hard to rebuild again later.

values.name: SecureEmailSent

values.name: SecureEmailReceivedUnread
values.raw: 15 Email received via EmailHostingAPI.  Has not been read.

values.name: SecureEmailReceivedRead
values.raw: 16 Email sent via EmailHostingAPI.  has been read.

values.raw: 17 Email failed to send.

values.name: Partial
values.raw: 3 - Some claims for this ERA have had financial information entered, no finalaized claim payment.

values.name: NotFinalized
values.raw: 4 - Ignores manually detached.  All claims for this ERA have had financial information entered, no finalaized claim payment.

values.name: FinalizedSomeDetached
values.raw: 5 - Some claims have been manually detached but all other claims have had financial information entered and finalaized claim payment created.

values.name: FinalizedAllDetached

values.name: OutNetwork
values.raw: 2, Formerly named "Allowed"

values.name: Phone
values.raw:
The value is a telephone number used for voice calls. Use of full international numbers starting with + is recommended to enable
            automatic dialing support but not required.

values.name: Fax
values.raw:
The value is a fax machine. Use of full international numbers starting with + is recommended to enable automatic dialing support 
            but not required.

values.name: Email
values.raw: The value is an email address.

values.name: Pager
values.raw: The value is a pager number. These may be local pager numbers that are only usable on a particular pager system.

values.name: Other
values.raw:
A contact that is not a phone, fax, or email address. The format of the value SHOULD be a URL. This is intended for various personal 
            contacts including blogs, Twitter, Facebook, etc. Do not use for email addresses. If this is not a URL, then it will require human 
            interpretation.

values.name: Home
values.raw:
A communication contact point at a home; attempted contacts for business purposes might intrude privacy and chances are one will 
            contact family or other household members instead of the person one wishes to call. Typically used with urgent cases, or if no other contacts 
            are available.

values.name: Work
values.raw: An office contact point. First choice for business related contacts during business hours.

values.name: Temp
values.raw: A temporary contact point. The period can provide more detailed information.

values.name: Old
values.raw: This contact point is no longer in use (or was never correct, but retained for records).

values.name: Mobile
values.raw:
A telecommunication device that moves and stays with its owner. May have characteristics of all other use codes, suitable for urgent 
            matters, not the first choice for routine business.

values.name: Requested
values.raw: The client has requested the subscription, and the server has not yet set it up.

values.name: Active
values.raw: The subscription is active.

values.name: Error
values.raw: The server has an error executing the notification.

values.name: Off
values.raw: Too many errors have occurred or the subscription has expired.

values.name: Rest_Hook
values.raw:
The channel is executed by making a post to the URI. If a payload is included, the URL is interpreted as the service base, and an 
            update (PUT) is made.

values.name: Websocket
values.raw:
The channel is executed by sending a packet across a web socket connection maintained by the client. The URL identifies the 
            websocket, and the client binds to this URL.

values.name: Email
values.raw: The channel is executed by sending an email to the email addressed in the URI (which must be a mailto:).

values.name: Sms
values.raw: The channel is executed by sending an SMS message to the phone number identified in the URL (tel:).

values.name: Message
values.raw:
The channel is executed by sending a message (e.g. a Bundle with a MessageHeader resource etc.) to the application identified in 
            the URI.

values.name: eCWFull
values.raw: Message structure is identical to eCWTight, minor changes in the program like showing patient demographics and the account module.Like eCWTight, eCW dictates the patients' PatNums in PID.2, so we try to locate the patient with that PatNum.If not found we do not attempt to use PID.4 ChartNumber or name, we assume new patient and insert.

values.name: eCWStandalone
values.raw: Only Incoming ADT messages are processed, OD is responsible for adding patients so we assign PatNum.The incoming messages patient ID in PID.2 is stored as ChartNumber and PID.4 is not processed.The Account and Chart modules are visible and the users can change and add patients in OD.  No outgoing messages.

values.name: eCWTight
values.raw: Patient demographics are hidden as well as account and appt modules.We let eCW dictate the PatNum values in PID.2 and trust that they are unique and longs (no string characters).Unlike Standalone, if the pat isn't found by PID.2 PatNum we don't try to locate the pat by PID.4 ChartNumber or name, we assume it's a new pat.

values.name: Centricity
values.raw: Account and Appointment modules are visible and users can change and add patients.Only outgoing DFT message defined, no incoming messages are processed.

values.name: HL7v2_6
values.raw: Our default behavior for processing and sending HL7 messages.Send and receive ADT and SIU messages, receive DFT messages.The v2.6 documentation claims both PID.2 and PID.4 are only retained for backward compatibility and PID.3 is now required and used for a list of patient IDs.We will still put ChartNumber in PID.2 (used to be referred to as 'external ID' by HL7 doc) for outgoing msgs and look for our PatNum in PID.2 for incoming msgs.We will now also check PID.3 for a repitition that contains our PatNum as part of the CX data type.Account and Appointments module are visible and users can change and add patients.

values.name: MedLabv2_3
values.raw:
This is currently used for LabCorp and is based on HL7 version 2.3 specifications.
            This interface has been built to the LabCorp standards and may not match the HL7 version 2.3 specs exactly.

values.name: A04
values.raw:
Only used with ADT/ACK.
            A04 - Register a PatientFor eCW, the A04 and A08 are inbound messages and are processed the same.  We attempt to locate the patient, if not found we insert one.For other interfaces, the same method of locating a patient and if not found inserting one will be used for inbound ADT^A04 and ADT^A08 messages.Outbound messages will have an A04 event type if a new patient is added

values.name: A08
values.raw:
Only used with ADT/ACK.
            A08 - Update Patient InfoA04 and A08 inbound are processed the same, but we will send the A08 event type if updating a patient in an outbound ADT.

values.name: P03
values.raw: Only used with DFT/ACK.

values.name: PC1
values.raw:
Only used with PPR/ACK (Patient Problem messages).
            Not used for eCW.These are inbound messages for adding/updating patient problems.PC1 is the Add event.  Add and Update events will be handled the same for now.

values.name: PC2
values.raw:
Only used with PPR/ACK (Patient Problem messages).
            Not used for eCW.PC2 is the Update event.  Add and Update events will be handled the same for now.

values.name: S03
values.raw:
Only used with SRM/SRR/ACK.  S03 - Request Appointment Modification.
            Not used for eCW.These will be inbound and are used for updating a limited amount of information for an existing appointment.S03 messages are used to update appointments.

values.name: S04
values.raw:
Only used with SRM/SRR/ACK.  S04 - Request Appointment Cancellation.
            Not used for eCW.S04 messages are used to set an appointment.AptStatus to ApptStatus.Broken.

values.name: S12
values.raw:
Only used with SIU/ACK.
            S12 - New ApptFor eCW, these are inbound, OD is considered an auxiliary application, and S12 and S14 messages are processed the same.For interfaces that require outbound SIU messages, OD is considered the filler application since OD has control over the operatories and schedules.As the filler application, events S12-S26 are the message events and they all have the same structure defined by HL7.Different actions in OD will cause a different outbound event type to be inserted, but the defined segments and fields will otherwise be the same.

values.name: S13
values.raw:
Only used with SIU/ACK.
            S13 - Appt Rescheduling

values.name: S14
values.raw:
Only used with SIU/ACK.
            S14 - Appt Modification

values.name: S15
values.raw:
Only used with SIU/ACK.
            S15 - Appt Cancellation

values.name: S17
values.raw:
Only used with SIU/ACK.
            S17 - Appt Deletion

values.name: NotDefined
values.raw: Use this for unsupported message structures

values.name: ADT_A01
values.raw:
Used for ADT/ACK event types A01, A04, A08, and A13.
            We currently only support A04 and A08 event types, both will use this structure.

values.name: DFT_P03
values.raw:
Used for DFT/ACK event type P03.
            All outbound DFT's are this structure.

values.name: ORU_R01
values.raw:
Used for ORU/ACK event type R01.
            All inbound ORU - Unsolicited transmission of an observation message will use this structure.This is used for all inbound LabCorp messages.

values.name: PPR_PC1
values.raw:
Used for PPR/ACK event types PC1, PC2, and PC3.
            We currently only support PC1 (add problem) and PC2 (update problem), both use this structure.

values.name: SIU_S12
values.raw:
Used for SIU/ACK event types S12 through S24 and S26.
            We currently only support S12 through S17.Inbound SIU's are all treated the same, regardless of the event type.We send different event types in outbound SIU's depending on the action that causes the message.All SIU's, inbound or outbound, use this message structure.

values.name: SRM_S01
values.raw:
Used for SRM/ACK event types S01 through S11.
            We currently only support S03 (update appt request) and S04 (cancel appt request).SRM's are inbound and when the action of updating or cancelling the appt is completed, an SRR is sent.SRM's and SRR's still require ACK's.  i.e. SRM received, ACK sent, action completed leads to SRR sent, ACK received.SRM's and SRR's will all use this message structure.

values.name: SRR_S01
values.raw:
Used for SRR/ACK event types S01 through S11.
            We currently only support S03 and S04 (see SRM_S01).SRR's are outbound and sent when an SRM is processed correctly.

values.name: None
values.raw: Default.

values.name: CI_CheckinPatient
values.raw:
Check-in a patient on a given device. For this type, the tag will have 3 items: the first name, last name, and birthdate of the patient in that order. 
            The birthdate will be in DateTime.Ticks.

values.name: CI_AddSheet
values.raw:
Tells the device that is currently filling out sheets to add a sheet to the list. For this type, the list of primary keys will have two items: the patnum and
            the SheetNum in that order.

values.name: CI_RemoveSheet
values.raw:
Tells the device that is currently fillout out sheets to remove a sheet from the list. For this type, the list of primary keys will have two items: the patnum
            and the SheetNum in that order.

values.name: CI_GoToCheckin
values.raw:
This mobile notification tells the device to stop whatever it is doing and go to a fresh checkin page. This may be a blank self-checkin or may be waiting for a mobile notification. This
            allows users from OD to "clear" the device of a stale patient. No primary keys or tags needed.

values.name: CI_NewEClipboardPrefs
values.raw:
This mobile notification occurs when the preferences for this device's clinic changes. The tags for this mobile notification will be the EClipboardAllowSelfCheckIn(bool), EClipboardMessageComplete(string), 
            EClipboardAllowSelfPortraitOnCheckIn(bool), and EClipboardPresentAvailableFormsOnCheckIn(bool) in that order.

values.name: IsAllowedChanged
values.raw:
This mobile notification occurs when the MobileAppDevice.IsAllowed changed for this device. The tag for this mobile notification will be IsAllowed (bool).
            If true then device which is currently awaiting in 'Not Allowed' state will try another login, should work this time. If false then force signout. Used for eClipboard and ODTouch.

values.name: ODM_LogoutODUser
values.raw:
This mobile notification occurs when a permission has changed for a given OD user and they are no longer allowed to use OD Mobile. 
            The ListPrimaryKeys may contain the UserNum of the user who is no longer allowed. This session will then be logged out of versioned OD Mobile.
            If ListPrimaryKeys IsNullOrEmpty() then assume all users for the given ClinicNum should be logged out. No UserNum filter necessary in this case.

values.name: CI_TreatmentPlan
values.raw:
This mobile notification occurs when a OD proper user sends a patients treatment plan to a specific device to show the user.
            ListPrimaryKeys => [MobileDataByteNum, PatNum, TreatPlanNum].
            ListTags Keys => The treatPlan.Heading, hasPracticeSig(Obsolete; based on if TP sheet has SigBoxPractice) .

values.name: CI_RemoveTreatmentPlan
values.raw:
This mobile notification occurs when a TreatmentPlan is deleted in OD and we want to tell a specific device so that they can remove it when viewing TreatmentPlans.
            ListPrimaryKeys => [TreatPlan.PatNum,TreatPlan.TreatPlanNum]

values.name: CI_SendPayment
values.raw:
This mobile notification occurs when a payment needs to be made on an eClip device. This either adds the Make Payment action item to the checkin checklist
            or it will open the QR code to scan from OD.
            ListPrimaryKeys => [TreatPlan.PatNum]

values.name: CI_RefreshPayment
values.raw:
This mobile notification occurs when a patient is currently on the device, when a payment is made, when a new card is added (XWeb only), and when a new 
            statement is created in OD.
            ListPrimaryKeys => [PatNum]

values.name: CI_PaymentPlan
values.raw:
This mobile notification occurs when an OD proper user sends a payment plan to a specific device.
            ListPrimaryKeys => [MobileDataByte.MobileDataByteNum,PayPlan.PatNum,PayPlan.PayPlanNum]
            ListTags => [PayPlan.PayPlanDate]

values.name: CI_RemovePaymentPlan
values.raw:
This mobile notification occurs when a payment plan is removed from the associated eClip device or when a payment plan is removed from OD proper.
            This will remove a payment plan from user view on eClip.
            ListPrimaryKeys => [PayPlan.PatNum,PayPlan.PayPlanNum]

values.name: ODM_NewTextMessage
values.raw: Occurs when a new text message is received. This is a workaround due to android push notifications no longer being supported for xamarin.

values.name: CI_AddEForm
values.raw: Tells the device that is currently filling out forms to add an eForm to the list. For this type, the list of primary keys will have two items: the PatNum and the EFormNum in that order.

values.name: CI_RemoveEForm
values.raw: Tells the device that is currently filling out forms to remove an eForm from the list. For this type, the list of primary keys will have two items: the PatNum and the EFormNum in that order.

We set this for PaySuitePayments that get inserted or updated with a PaymentStatus of "P" (Paid) and those that change from "P" to "R" (Reversed). values.name: TimedOut
values.raw: 3 - Pearl did not give results within the timeout period of 10 minutes. Polling for this request can be retried.

values.name: ErrorProcessing

values.name: NoResults

values.name: None
values.raw:
-1 - None: Used for marking trans that represent account items NOT sent to Transworld.  The TsiTransLogs with this trans type are used
            to prevent the ODService from sending the trans to Transworld.  Example: Transworld collects a payment from a patient and deduct their fee and
            send the practice the remaining amt.  Office staff will enter the payment in the patient's account and then enter an adjustment to account for
            Transworld's fee.  TsiTransLogs will be inserted for the paysplits and adjustment so that we don't send those trans to Transworld and cause an
            infinite loop of sorts.  Logs with this type will be update by the ODService if the ledger trans amt is updated.

values.name: CN
values.raw: 0 - Cancel: cancel collection services for an account. Collection services can be restarted but will incur another TSI fee.

values.name: CR
values.raw: 1 - Credit Adjustment: negative adjustment to reduce balance. Example: a discount given or portion of the debt written off.

values.name: DB
values.raw:
2 - Debit Adjustment: positive adjustment to increase balance.  Offices are supposed to stop all finance charges once placed with TSI,
            but there may be other transactions that require increasing the amount owed.

values.name: PF
values.raw: 3 - Paid in Full: payment entered that pays off account balance.  Closes account with TSI and stops collection activity.

values.name: PL
values.raw: 4 - Placement: account sent to TSI for Accelerator/Profit Recovery/Collection services.

values.name: PP
values.raw: 5 - Partial Payment: payment by either patient or ins payment/writeoff that pays a portion of the balance.

values.name: PT
values.raw:
6 - Paid in Full, Thank You: payment entered that pays off account balance.  Closes account with TSI and stops collection activity.
            TSI will send a Thank You letter to the patient free of charge.

values.name: RI
values.raw:
7 - Reinstate: an account that has been suspended can be reinstated within 50 days and the collection services will resume where it
            left off.  After 50 days the account is automatically cancelled and in order to restart collection services the office would have to initiate a
            new placement, which will incur another TSI fee.

values.name: SS
values.raw:
8 - Suspend: places collection services for the account on hold for up to 50 days.  Example: After an account is placed with TSI, the
            customer comes into the office and agrees to a payment plan.  The account can be suspended and if the patient fails to make a payment within 50
            days the account can be reinstated and the collection process will resume where it left off and TSI will not charge an additional fee.  After
            50 days the account is automatically cancelled by TSI and in order to restart the collection process, the office would have to initiate a new
            placement which starts the collection process over and will result in an additional TSI fee.

values.name: Agg
values.raw: 9 - To differentiate aggregate rows from rows linked to transactions in the OD db.

values.name: Excluded
values.raw:
10 - Used for adjustments entered with the SyncExcludePosAdjType or SyncExcludeNegAdjType set in the Transworld program link.
            Excluded from syncing with TSI and from the amount due calculation used in future msgs or to determine if the acct is paid in full.

values.name: Adjustment
values.raw:
0 - adjustment.AdjNum.  Can be a positive (Debit) or negative (Credit) adjustment to the amount owed.  The resulting message
            TsiTransType is DB (Debit) for positive adjustments or CR (Credit) for negative adjustments.

values.name: Claimproc
values.raw:
1 - claimproc.ClaimProcNum. For ins payments and/or writeoffs entered after the account has been placed with TSI.  The resulting
            message TsiTransType is PP (Partial Payment), PF (Paid in Full), or PT (Paid in Full, Thank You).

values.name: PayPlan
values.raw:
2 - payplan.PayPlanNum.  In payplan version 1 the entire CompletedAmt is aged, so it will be negative (credit) and decrease the amount
            owed.  The resulting message TsiTransType is CR (Credit).

values.name: PayPlanCharge
values.raw:
3 - payplancharge.PayPlanChargeNum.  Depends on payplan version, could be negative (credit - decrease amount owed) or
            positive (debit - increase amount owed).  The resulting message TsiTransType is DB (Debit) if positive or CR (Credit) if negative.

values.name: PaySplit
values.raw:
4 - paysplit.SplitNum.  Patient payment on an account placed with TSI.  The resulting message TsiTransType is PP (Partial Payment),
            PF (Paid in Full), or PT (Paid in Full, Thank You).

values.name: Procedure
values.raw: 5 - procedurelog.ProcNum.  Positive (debit, increases the amount owed).  The resulting message TsiTransType is DB (Debit).

values.name: Invalid
values.raw: -1 - Do not use.

values.name: Preferred
values.raw: 0 - Use text OR email based on patient preference.

values.name: Text
values.raw: 1 - Attempt to send text message, if successful do not send via email. (Unless, a SendAll bool is used, which usually negates the need for this enumeration.)

values.name: Email
values.raw: 2 - Attempt to send email message, if successful do not send via text. (Unless, a SendAll bool is used, which usually negates the need for this enumeration.)

values.name: SecureEmail
values.raw: 3 - Attempt to send secure email message.

values.name: ZeroDollarAuthApproval

values.name: ExpiredWithoutApproval
values.raw: 101 - Expired Without Approval.  Hosted Form timed out without Approval, OTK was never launched, or Invalid OTK

values.name: Pending
values.raw: 102 - Pending (neither of the above events has occurred yet)

values.name: ParsingError
values.raw: 800 Parsing Error Unable to parse the XML request sent.

values.name: MaxRequestDataExceededError
values.raw: 801 Maximum Request Data Exceeded Error - The XML request exceeds the 2048-byte maximum size.

values.name: DuplicateFieldError
values.raw: 802 Duplicate Field Error - The XML request had more than one copy of a particular field. The field causing the error may be specified.

values.name: ImproperDLLError
values.raw: 803 Improper DLL Error - Unrecognized DLL name. This can be caused by a wrong URL entered into the "Server Location" setting under XCharge Server Setup, Credit Cards, Connection.

values.name: SpecificationVersionError
values.raw: 804 Specification Version Error - XML error, the Specification Version field is set incorrectly.

values.name: AuthenticationError
values.raw: 805 Authentication Error -  The XWeb ID, Auth Key or Terminal ID fields are incorrect (check for leading and trailing spaces if they appear to match those on file). The field causing the error may be specified.

values.name: ProductionMerchantSetUpError
values.raw: 806 Production Merchant Set Up Error - The Mode was incorrectly sent. This can happen when trying to process on theProduction server with a Development or Test Mode Processing Account.

values.name: TestMerchantSetUpError
values.raw: 807 Test Merchant Set Up Error - The Mode was incorrectly sent. This can happen when trying to process on the Testserver with a Development or Production Mode Processing Account.

values.name: DevelopmentMerchantSetUpError
values.raw: 808 Development Merchant Set Up Error - The Mode was incorrectly sent. This can happen when trying to process on theDevelopment server with a Production or Test Mode Processing Account.

values.name: RequiredFieldNotSentError
values.raw: 809 Required Field Not Sent Error - A field that is required for this transaction type was not sent. The field causing theerror may be specified.

values.name: InconsistentConditionalFieldError
values.raw: 810 Inconsistent Conditional Field Error - A field that does not have to be sent was sent in the wrong context. The field causing the error may be specified.

values.name: ImproperFieldDataError
values.raw: 811 Improper Field Data Error - A field sent to the EdgeExpress Gateway was not formatted correctly. This could pertain to Processing Account Information configured in XCharge or BMS, or card information. The field causing the error may be specified.

values.name: UnrecognizedNameTagError
values.raw: 812 Unrecognized Name / Tag Error - The XML tag sent is not in the API

values.name: DuplicateTransactionError
values.raw: 813 Duplicate Transaction Error - A transaction was run for the same amount on the same card within a certain time limit. The duplicate checking time is set on the EdgeExpress Gateway, usuallybetween 1 and 60 minutes.

values.name: InvalidReferenceError
values.raw: 814 Invalid Reference Error - The Transaction ID used for a Void, Return, etc. is invalid.

values.name: TransactionAlreadyVoided
values.raw: 815 Transaction Already Voided - The Transaction ID used for a Void was already voided.

values.name: TransactionAlreadyCaptured
values.raw: 816 Transaction Already Captured - The Transaction ID used for a Capture of an Authorized charge was already used and the transaction has been Captured.

values.name: EmptyBatch
values.raw: 817 Empty Batch - The batch is empty and cannot settle. You cannot settle an empty batch.

values.name: MerchantLockedForSettlement
values.raw: 818 Merchant Locked For Settlement - The Processing Account is in the process of being settled/batched. Wait a moment and try again.

values.name: MerchantLockedForMaintenance
values.raw: 819 Merchant Locked for Maintenance - The Processing Account is locked for database or server maintenance. Wait amoment and try again.

values.name: TemporaryServiceOutage
values.raw: 820 Temporary Service Outage - Retry Transaction - The EdgeExpress Gateway itself may be down. Wait a moment and try again.

values.name: ProcessingHostUnavailable
values.raw: 821 Processing Host Unavailable - Certain back end account parameters may not be set correctly. Have the account settings checked. Specifically, check the TSYS Vital Hierarchy Values.

values.name: InvalidAccountData
values.raw: 823 Invalid Account Data - A field sent to the EdgeExpress Gateway appears invalid (correctly formatted but not on file). This could pertain to Processing Account Information configured in XCharge or BMS, or card information. The field causing the response may be specified.

values.name: IndustryMismatchError
values.raw: 824 Industry Mismatch Error - The Processing Account is configured with the incorrect Market Type. This can occur if XCharge or BMS is not configured with the same Market Type as the EdgeExpress Gateway.

values.name: RejectedInternalSupportOnly
values.raw: 825 Rejected Internal support only -  Reserved for Fraud, Not currently implemented.

values.name: InvalidCardType
values.raw: 827 Invalid Card Type -  The account number entered is not valid for the card type entered. If Card Type is Visa, then the account number must be for a Visa account.

values.name: CardTypeNotSupported
values.raw: 828 Card Type Not Supported - The card type (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, etc.) for the attempted transaction is not enabled at the EdgeExpress Gateway.

values.name: CardCodeRequired
values.raw: 829 Card Code Required - The Card Security Code (CSC, also known as the CVV, CVC or CID) is set to "required" for keyed transactions at the EdgeExpress Gateway, but was not sent.

values.name: AddressRequired
values.raw: 830 Address Required - The address (house number, part of the Address Verification System) is set to "required" for keyed transactions at the EdgeExpress Gateway, but was not sent.

values.name: ZipCodeRequired
values.raw: 831 ZIP Code Required - The ZIP code (part of the Address Verification System) is set to "required" for keyed transactions at the EdgeExpress Gateway, but was not sent.

values.name: EncodedDataFormatError
values.raw: 832 Encoded Data Format Error - Encoded format of check image file could not be read or was not submitted when expected.

values.name: CheckServicesImageErrorMICRAndAmout
values.raw: 833 Check Services Image Error - MICR and Amount cannot be read The attempted paper check scan failed. When this occurs you should have the option to manually enter the check information or rescan the check.

values.name: CheckServicesImageErrorMICROnly
values.raw: 834 Check Services Image Error - MICR cannot be read The attempted paper check scan failed. When this occurs you should have the option to manually enter the check information or rescan the check.

values.name: CheckServicesImageErrorAmountOnly
values.raw: 835 Check Services Image Error - Amount cannot be read The attempted paper check scan failed. When this occurs you should have the option to manually enter the check information or rescan the check.

values.name: EmailServiceError
values.raw: 838 Email Service Error - The EdgeExpress Gateway attempted to send an email, possibly for a password reset request, but the attempt failed.

values.name: InvalidReferenceErrorResponseCodeMisMatch
values.raw: 842 Invalid Reference Error - Response code returned when the referenced transaction type does not match.

values.name: TSYSError
values.raw: 900 TSYS Error - Error thrown by Processor TSYS: the EdgeExpress Gateway is setup correctly, but the card or other value submitted is incorrect. SERV NOT ALLOWED usually indicates a Decline, Failure CV indicates the Card Type is not supported (e.g. AMEX needs to be enabled), and Failure HV indicates an error on the account setup in the EdgeExpress Gateway.

values.name: ProcessorError
values.raw: 901 Processor Error Can be Global or TSYS errors (900 Global Payments Error: or 900 TSYS Error:) are returned on transactions if a processor setting is not configured correctly. This can occur if the card or transaction type is not enabled on the Processing Account at the processor end.

values.name: None
